geographic_restrictions: Massachusetts (Lead Applicant must be based in Massachusetts; out-of-state partners permitted if funding directly benefits Massachusetts residents and organizations)
project_area: Clean energy workforce training and career pathways for underserved populations
demographic_requirements: Priority populations include individuals from Environmental Justice (EJ) neighborhoods, low-income communities, members of Federally recognized and State-acknowledged Tribes, Fossil Fuel Workers, and other underrepresented populations
eligibility_summary: Single organizations or partnerships are eligible to apply. Lead Applicants must be Massachusetts-based entities, including community-based organizations, educational institutions, trade/labor entities, tribes, workforce development organizations, or Massachusetts Workforce Investment Boards. Applicants may apply for one or more of three strands: Career Pathway Training, Clean Energy Pipeline Training, or Planning/Capacity Projects. Projects must address barriers faced by priority populations, demonstrate employer engagement, and leverage existing workforce development systems.
